implementation_summary.delete_edit_rumor_kind: Exported DELETE_EDIT_RUMOR_KIND = 5 constant, mirroring reactions/rumor.ts's REACTION_KIND=7 module-local pattern but exported per this story's explicit instruction (S3/S4/S5 all need it to classify inbound/outbound kind-5 rumors).
implementation_summary.build_delete_rumor: buildDeleteRumor(originalId, priorReplacementIds[], targetKind, rev, selfPrivKeyHex) -> unmarked kind-5. Tags: ["e",originalId], one ["e",id] per prior replacement (AC-DEL-8), ["k",String(targetKind)]. created_at = rev directly (a delete's own created_at IS its rev). Never emits the ["e",...,"","edit"] marker (AC-DEL-7 substrate).
implementation_summary.build_edit_replacement_rumor: buildEditReplacementRumor(originalId, originalCreatedAt, content, targetKind, rev, selfPrivKeyHex) -> kind-14 (targetKind=14) or kind-9 (targetKind=9). Wire created_at FIELD (seconds) is pinned to originalCreatedAt (the caller-supplied FIRST message's created_at across the whole edit chain, AC-EDIT-4/AC-EDIT-6) and is never derived from rev. rev rides separately as ["rev",String(rev)] alongside ["e",originalId,"","edit"].
implementation_summary.build_edit_marked_companion_kind5: buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5(originalId, priorReplacementIds[], targetKind, rev, selfPrivKeyHex) -> marked kind-5 for non-Few degradation only. Same e-tag chain as buildDeleteRumor (original + every prior replacement, plus k-tag) with an appended ["e",originalId,"","edit"] marker — the original id therefore appears both bare and marked, which is intentional (see design_decisions).
implementation_summary.clamp_rev: clampRev(wallClockSeconds, lastKnownRevForSlot) -> max(safeWall, safeLast+1), where safeWall/safeLast are floor-substituted (1 / 0 respectively) for any non-finite or out-of-range input BEFORE the max is taken — guarantees a return value that is always finite and >= 1, matching the S1 storage clobber-guard's 'rev>=1 == real signal' convention exactly (D16, AC-ORDER-5).
implementation_summary.shared_id_discipline: All three rumor-returning builders funnel through a private finalizeRumor(kind, content, tags, created_at, selfPrivKeyHex) helper that derives pubkey via getPublicKey and computes the canonical id via getEventHash before returning — mirrors reactions/rumor.ts's build-once-canonical-id discipline so the optimistic local id equals what the transport (sealAndWrap for DM, sendRumorSafe for group) will eventually publish, as long as the same privkey/content/tags/created_at/kind are used downstream.
implementation_summary.input_guards: Each builder throws synchronously on structurally invalid input (empty originalId/content, non-string priorReplacementIds entries, targetKind outside {9,14}, non-finite/negative originalCreatedAt, rev < 1 or non-finite, empty selfPrivKeyHex) — mirrors buildReactionRumor's own throw-on-invalid-input style.

design_decisions.decision: Added a trailing selfPrivKeyHex: string parameter to buildDeleteRumor, buildEditReplacementRumor, and buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5, beyond what the frozen DeleteEditRumor seam type in stories.json#seams literally lists.
design_decisions.rationale: UnsignedRumor.pubkey (app/src/lib/directMessages.ts:25-32) is a mandatory field, and getEventHash's canonical NIP-01 id serialization includes it — the listed seam shorthand (originalId/priorReplacementIds/targetKind/rev, or originalId/originalCreatedAt/content/targetKind/rev) has no parameter that can supply it, so as literally written the contract cannot produce a valid UnsignedRumor. This is a genuine gap in the frozen seam shorthand, not a naming/placement choice. Resolved by direct precedent already in the codebase: reactions/rumor.ts's buildReactionRumor takes exactly this parameter, and BOTH existing call sites — DM's publishDirectReaction (directMessages.ts:404) and group's ChatStoreContext.sendReaction (ChatStoreContext.tsx:364) — already pass their privateKeyHex to it. In other words, the single-builder-shared-by-both-transports pattern this story was told to mirror already solves this exact problem the same way. Both S4 and S5 already hold a privateKeyHex/pubkey in scope at their respective call sites (S4: directMessages.ts functions already take privateKeyHex as a parameter; S5: ChatStoreContext already threads privateKeyHex through to sendReaction), so the addition imposes no new plumbing burden downstream. Documented in architecture.json#deviations_from_frozen_seam_shorthand and flagged for downstream confirmation via post-impl questions VQ-S2-012 and VQ-S2-014.
design_decisions.scope_note: Not a scope violation — no new module boundary is crossed; the parameter is purely additive to a pure function's own signature. Behavior-neutral for the product: no UI or user-facing change, only an internal function-call shape.

design_decisions.decision: Typed targetKind as 9 | 14 (never 5) on all three rumor-returning builders, though stories.json#seams literally types buildDeleteRumor's targetKind as 5|9|14 and buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5's as a bare 5.
design_decisions.rationale: targetKind denotes the kind of the CHAT MESSAGE being deleted/edited (14 DM / 9 group) — it becomes the ["k", String(targetKind)] tag value per this story's own instruction text ('14 for DM, 9 for group'). It is never the signal's own kind, which is already fixed via DELETE_EDIT_RUMOR_KIND. The literal 5 entries in the seam JSON are treated as a copy/paste artifact (also inconsistent with buildEditReplacementRumor's own narrower 9|14 typing in the same seam JSON). Flagged as post-impl question VQ-S2-013.
design_decisions.scope_note: Tightens a type at implementation; behavior-neutral (S4/S5 already only ever pass 9 or 14).

design_decisions.decision: buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5's tag array intentionally contains the original id twice: once bare (["e",originalId], part of the AC-DEL-8 hide-chain shared with buildDeleteRumor) and once marked (["e",originalId,"","edit"], the AC-DEL-7 discriminator).
design_decisions.rationale: The story instructions specify the companion carries 'the same e-tag chain as the delete builder ... PLUS ["e", originalId, "", "edit"]' — i.e. additive, not a replacement of the bare tag. Flagged for S3's classification logic in post-impl question VQ-S2-015.
design_decisions.scope_note: No scope impact — wire-shape-only decision within this story's own builder.

design_decisions.decision: kind-5 rumors (delete and companion) are built with content: '' (no content/reason parameter exposed).
design_decisions.rationale: Neither the story's parameter list nor any AC calls for a delete/edit reason string; NIP-09 treats kind-5 content as an optional free-text reason, and no caller-facing use case for it exists in this epic. Kept out rather than speculatively added.

coverage: buildDeleteRumor: kind, unmarked (no edit-tag), e-tags original with zero priors, e-tags original + every prior replacement id (AC-DEL-8) with tag-count assertion, k-tag for DM (14) and group (9), created_at===rev, no sig field, valid pubkey

buildDeleteRumor: id matches independently-recomputed getEventHash; determinism (same inputs -> same id); varying priorReplacementIds/rev/originalId each change tags and id (anti-stub coverage per VQ-S2-001)
buildDeleteRumor input guards: empty originalId, empty-string entry in priorReplacementIds, invalid targetKind, rev<1, non-finite rev (NaN/Infinity), empty selfPrivKeyHex

buildEditReplacementRumor: kind 14/9, content is new text, wire created_at equals ORIGINAL created_at not rev (AC-EDIT-4), edit anchor tag present, separate rev tag matches passed rev, no bare (unmarked) e-tag present, id matches recomputed getEventHash, determinism, varying content/rev each change id while created_at stays pinned

buildEditReplacementRumor AC-EDIT-6: 3-edit chain test asserting every replacement e-tags/pins to the FIRST message's id/created_at, explicitly asserting NON-reference to the immediately-prior edit's id
buildEditReplacementRumor input guards: empty originalId, empty content, negative/non-finite originalCreatedAt, invalid targetKind, rev<1

buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5: kind 5, edit marker tag present, full e-tag chain (original+priors) plus marker (4-tag-count assertion for 2 priors), k-tag for DM/group, tag-shape and id differ from the unmarked delete for identical inputs, id matches recomputed getEventHash, determinism
buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5 input guards: empty originalId, rev<1, invalid targetKind

clampRev: table-driven it.each across ordinary-forward-tick, equal-clock, stale-clock, future-skewed-clock, no-prior-rev, and floor-to-1 cases; monotonic-per-slot sequence test (5-call chain, each result feeds the next lastKnownRevForSlot, strictly increasing); non-finite/negative-input guard table (11 malformed-input cases: zero/negative/NaN/Infinity/-Infinity on either argument) each asserting finite and >=1

gates.test_status: PASS (124 files, 2197 tests; rumor.test.ts alone: 67 -> 84 tests)
gates.lint_status: NO_LINT (unchanged from baseline — no eslint config present in this project)

verification_json.note: Pre-impl questions VQ-S2-001..011 left untouched per the immutability rule. Appended VQ-S2-012 (selfPrivKeyHex parameter addition and whether it's the only way to satisfy UnsignedRumor.pubkey given the frozen seam), VQ-S2-013 (targetKind:9|14 vs the seam JSON's literal 5 entries), VQ-S2-014 (clampRev's floor-before-max guard never producing <1/non-finite, tied to S1's clobber-guard dependency), VQ-S2-015 (buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5's intentional duplicate original e-tag, bare+marked, and whether S3's classifier tolerates it).

remediation_history.trigger: Two independent model reviews found defense-in-depth gaps in the Wire builders module (all below the ship-blocking floor, closed before S4/S6 call these builders).

findings_addressed.finding: Ms-vs-seconds hazard in buildEditReplacementRumor: originalCreatedAt only checked finite && >=0, so a caller passing ChatMessage.createdAt (milliseconds) or an unfloored /1000 (fractional) would produce a wire created_at ~1000x in the future or a NIP-01-illegal fractional value, pinned across the whole edit chain.
findings_addressed.fix: originalCreatedAt now requires Number.isInteger(...) and must be below a new MAX_PLAUSIBLE_UNIX_SECONDS=100_000_000_000 ceiling; a value at/above the ceiling throws a clear 'looks like milliseconds, expected Unix seconds' error.

findings_addressed.finding: assertRealRev (and thus the rev parameter of all three builders) only required finite && >=1, so a caller bypassing clampRev (e.g. unfloored Date.now()/1000) could put a fractional created_at / ["rev", "...5"] tag on the wire — both NIP-01 violations.
findings_addressed.fix: assertRealRev now requires Number.isInteger(rev) && rev >= 1. clampRev itself is unaffected (already integer-returning); only the direct-rev bypass path is tightened.

findings_addressed.finding: clampRev test gaps: no fractional-input cases with pinned exact integer results, no Number.isInteger assertion in the malformed-input table, no pinned exact values proving strictly-greater (not just >=1) semantics under malformed input.
findings_addressed.fix: Added fractional wallClockSeconds/lastKnownRevForSlot cases with pinned exact integer expectations; added Number.isInteger assertion to the malformed-input table; added a dedicated describe block pinning exact results for NaN/negative wall or last against a well-formed counterpart (e.g. clampRev(NaN, 100) === 101).

findings_addressed.finding: Existing tests used toContainEqual + counts, so a stray extra tag (including one leaking key material) would pass undetected.
findings_addressed.fix: Added one expect(rumor.tags).toEqual([...exact ordered array...]) test per builder (buildDeleteRumor, buildEditReplacementRumor, buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5) pinning the exact wire tag shape and order.

findings_addressed.finding: priorReplacementIds was not deduplicated and originalId was not excluded from it, so overlapping ids yielded duplicate e-tags and changed the canonical id for semantically identical input.
findings_addressed.fix: Added a shared dedupeIdsKeepFirst(originalId, priorReplacementIds) helper (Set-based, originalId first) used by both buildDeleteRumor and buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5's bare e-tag chain. The companion's intentional bare+marked originalId duplication (its own separate, story-instructed appended marker tag) is untouched. Added dedup tests for both builders, including an id-equivalence assertion against the pre-deduplicated input.

not_changed_per_brief: buildEditMarkedCompanionKind5's bare + marked originalId duplication (deliberate, carried to S3 as a marker-first classifier obligation, tracked separately)

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The cross-story seam contract frozen in stories.json#seams (DeleteEditRumor) omits any parameter for the sender's key material, even though the return type (UnsignedRumor) mandates a `pubkey` field that getEventHash's canonical id computation depends on. As literally specified, the seam cannot produce a valid rumor. I resolved this by adding a trailing selfPrivKeyHex parameter, using the exact same resolution the codebase already applies to an analogous problem (reactions/rumor.ts's buildReactionRumor, called identically by both DM and group transports today) — but a downstream story author trusting the seam JSON verbatim would have hit the same wall. The seam JSON also has a likely copy/paste artifact (targetKind typed as bare `5` in two of the four listed signatures) that a naive implementation would have propagated as an over-permissive type.
retrospective.surprised_by: The story-planner's stories.json#seams shorthand is treated as the authoritative, frozen cross-story contract per this agent's own instructions, yet in this case it was incomplete in a way only visible by cross-checking the actual UnsignedRumor type definition and the existing reactions/rumor.ts precedent — the seam JSON alone did not surface the gap.
